short: An 8-bit floating-point format that cuts memory and boosts throughput on modern GPUs.
definition: FP8 is an 8-bit floating-point number format supported by recent GPUs that uses eight bits per value instead of the historical sixteen or thirty-two. It roughly halves memory and can double throughput for both training and inference, at the cost of careful per-tensor scaling to stay accurate. It is increasingly used in large-model training pipelines and optimized serving engines.
